<Meta of={TableStories} name="Overview" />

# Tables

The table component provides accessible, responsive data tables with consistent styling. Tables automatically adapt to different screen sizes and integrate with the design system's theming.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to see how tables adapt to different section themes.

<div className="sb-contained-canvas">
  <Canvas of={TableStories.Table} />
</div>

## Properties

Tables are rendered using standard HTML table markup. The component automatically applies appropriate styling based on the section theme context.

### Structure

Tables should include all semantic HTML elements:

- `<table>`: Container element
- `<thead>`: Table header section
- `<tbody>`: Table body section
- `<tfoot>`: Table footer section (optional)
- `<tr>`: Table rows
- `<th>`: Header cells
- `<td>`: Data cells

## Table Features

### Responsive Behavior

Tables automatically become scrollable on small screens to prevent content from becoming truncated or unreadable.

### Striped Rows

Alternating row colors improve readability for long tables.

### Header Styling

Table headers receive distinct styling to clearly separate them from data cells.

### Cell Alignment

- Text content: Left-aligned by default
- Numeric data: Right-aligned for easy comparison
- Headers: Can be aligned to match cell content

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Atom
- **Template**: `example-tables.twig`
- **Styles**: `cl-table.scss`
- **JavaScript**: `table.js`

### Accessibility

- **Semantic HTML**: Uses proper table elements (`<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<th>`, `<td>`)
- **Header Association**: Column headers use `<th>` with scope attributes
- **Row Headers**: Use `<th scope="row">` for row headers when applicable
- **Caption**: Tables should include `<caption>` for context
- **Summary**: Complex tables include summary attribute or description
- **Keyboard Navigation**: Full keyboard accessibility for scrollable tables
- **Screen Readers**: Proper markup allows screen readers to navigate table structure

### Responsive Behavior

- **Small Screens**: Tables become horizontally scrollable
- **Scroll Indicators**: Visual cues show when table extends beyond viewport
- **Touch Friendly**: Scrolling works smoothly on touch devices
- **Sticky Headers**: Optional sticky header behavior for long tables

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use tables for tabular data only, not for layout
- Include a descriptive caption that summarizes table content
- Use `<th>` for all header cells with appropriate scope attributes
- Keep tables simple - avoid nested tables
- Left-align text, right-align numbers for easy scanning
- Provide adequate cell padding for readability
- Sort data in a logical order (alphabetical, chronological, etc.)
- Highlight important data with bold or background colors
- For large tables, consider adding filters or search functionality

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use tables for page layout - use CSS Grid or Flexbox
- Avoid overly wide tables that require excessive horizontal scrolling
- Don't merge cells unnecessarily - keeps tables accessible
- Avoid putting too much content in a single cell
- Don't use color alone to convey meaning
- Avoid very long tables without pagination or virtual scrolling
- Don't use tables when a list or other format would be clearer

## Implementation Notes

### Basic Table Structure

```html
<table>
  <caption>
    Student Grade Report
  </caption>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th scope="col">Student Name</th>
      <th scope="col">Assignment</th>
      <th scope="col">Grade</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th scope="row">Jane Smith</th>
      <td>Essay 1</td>
      <td>95</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th scope="row">John Doe</th>
      <td>Essay 1</td>
      <td>87</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

### Responsive Tables

For tables with many columns, consider these responsive strategies:

1. **Horizontal Scrolling**: Allow table to scroll horizontally (default behavior)
2. **Priority Columns**: Show most important columns on mobile, hide others
3. **Stacked Layout**: Transform table rows into cards on mobile (requires custom CSS)

### Table Sorting

Add sorting functionality for enhanced usability:

```html
<th scope="col" class="sortable">
  <button type="button" aria-sort="none">Column Name</button>
</th>
```

### Theming

Tables inherit colors from the section theme context. Cell backgrounds, borders, and text colors automatically adjust to ensure proper contrast.

## When to Use Tables

Tables are appropriate for:

- Comparing items across multiple dimensions
- Showing precise numerical data
- Displaying structured data with clear relationships
- Financial reports and data analysis
- Schedules and timetables
- Product comparison charts

## When Not to Use Tables

Consider alternatives for:

- Layout purposes (use CSS Grid/Flexbox)
- Lists of items (use `<ul>` or `<ol>`)
- Simple key-value pairs (use definition lists)
- Forms (use proper form elements)
- Navigation menus (use nav elements)

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all table variations and theme combinations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/atoms-table-visreg).
